message:
fix(inet/net_pton): match libresolv inet_net_ntop format exactly + diff harness

Two coupled changes that bring frankenlibc's libresolv-compatible
CIDR codec into byte-for-byte parity with the host glibc on
inet_net_ntop and pin it with a differential conformance harness.

inet/net_pton.rs::format
------------------------
The previous format() implementation took two libresolv-incompatible
shortcuts:

1. Suppressed the `/prefix` suffix when prefix_bits == 32 AND the
   address was a 4-byte form ("host address shorthand"). libresolv's
   inet_net_ntop ALWAYS emits the `/prefix` suffix; the shorthand
   only applies to inet_ntop, not inet_net_ntop. This made fl's
   format("/32") output diverge from glibc on a wide range of
   common inputs.

2. Emitted nothing when bytes_needed was zero (e.g. prefix_bits == 0
   with empty source). libresolv emits at least one octet ("0/0")
   so the output never starts with '/'.

Plus a third subtler defect: format() never masked the final
partial-octet to the prefix boundary, so a /20 with bytes
[192, 168, 0xFF, 0] would render as "192.168.255/20" instead of
"192.168.240/20" (libresolv masks to /20 = top 20 bits of the
3rd octet → 0xF0, the rest is 0).

The fix:

  emit_count = bytes_needed.max(1)        // never start with '/'
  for i in 0..emit_count:
      byte = bytes.get(i).unwrap_or(0)
      if (i+1)*8 > prefix_bits > i*8:      // partial trailing byte
          kept_bits = prefix_bits - i*8
          mask = !0u8 << (8 - kept_bits)
          byte &= mask
      write_decimal(byte)
  out.push('/')
  write_decimal(prefix_bits)               // ALWAYS emit /prefix

Doc comment updated to reflect the conforming behavior — the prior
"omit /prefix when /32 and 4-byte" sentence is gone.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/conformance_diff_inet_net.rs (new, 207 LOC)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Diff harness mirroring the existing libresolv differential pattern
in this crate. Links libresolv.so.2 directly (`#[link(name="resolv")]`)
and runs both inet_net_pton AND inet_net_ntop side-by-side against
fl's `glibc_internal_abi::inet_net_pton` / `_ntop` exports across:

- Pure host-address forms ("10.0.0.1") at /8/16/24/32 prefixes
- Partial-octet forms ("192.168", "10") that libresolv expands
- Explicit /prefix forms with prefix < 32 ("192.168.0.0/16")
- Partial-prefix boundaries ("192.168.255.0/20" → masking matters)
- /0 ("0/0", "0.0.0.0/0")
- /32 host-form (must NOT be shorthand-elided through inet_net_ntop)
- Buffer too small (return value -1, errno preserved)

Fields compared: return value (prefix bits / -1), output buffer
contents, errno on failure. Divergences are collected into a
Divergence vec with function/case/field/fl/glibc columns and
panicked at the end so the failure dump shows every divergence
in one shot, not just the first.

Sister to the existing __b64_ntop / __b64_pton diff (6bf35d7b)
filed under bd-xn6p8 follow-up, broadening libresolv parity
coverage from the base64 codec to the address codec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
